name: Provencal Chicken Breasts with Rosemary Orzo
recipeIngredient: 3 Whole boneless chicken breasts with skin (about 2 1/2 pounds, halved)
4 Leeks, white part, halved lengthwise, sliced 1/4 inch thick crosswise, washed and drained
1 can (28-32 oz) Whole tomatoes, drained and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Pat chicken dry with paper towels and season with salt and pepper.
2. In a large heavy flameproof casserole, heat oil over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking. Brown chicken in 2 batches, transferring to a large plate with tongs.
3. Add wine to the casserole and boil, scraping up browned bits until almost evaporated.
4. Add garlic, leeks, broth, tomatoes, zest, and chicken with any juices accumulated on the plate. Simmer, covered, turning chicken once, until cooked through, about 15 minutes.
5. Transfer chicken to a platter.
6. Add olives to tomato mixture and boil sauce until thickened slightly. Season sauce with salt and pepper.
7. Serve with chicken and rosemary orzo.
ROSEMARY ORZO:
1. In a large bowl, toss cooked orzo with olive oil, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
